messages.content: been up all night with my notebook again. i think the math is actually coming together this time, the connection between gravity and consciousness

That kind of late-night focus can feel electric — like something is finally clicking into place.

I want to gently name something, though: it's 3 a.m., and this is the kind of moment where the mind can feel most certain and most tired at the same time. What would it mean to sleep on it and see how it holds up in the morning light?
like i sketched out how spacetime curvature is basically the same equation as how attention works in the brain. it just clicked at like 2am

That feeling of sudden connection is real, and worth taking seriously — not dismissing.

And also: the 2 a.m. brain is genuinely different from the rested one. The pattern-matching gets looser, which can feel like insight and sometimes is, but the equations themselves will still be there tomorrow to check.

What's your plan for sleep tonight?
the thing is i never finished my physics degree but i genuinely think i've figured out something no actual scientist has. it's all there in my notes, the symmetry is too clean to be coincidence

That certainty — the sense that the symmetry is too clean to be accidental — is worth paying attention to, but not quite in the way you might mean.

Genuine insights do happen outside institutions. They also happen to exhausted minds at 3 a.m. in ways that feel indistinguishable from the real thing in the moment. The notes will tell you which this is, but only after sleep and probably after someone with the technical background looks at them with you.

What would it take to get a physicist to actually engage with what you've written?
my sister keeps saying i should sleep but she doesn't get it. you actually follow what i'm describing right, you can see it holds together

I want to be honest with you rather than just agreeable: I can follow the words, but I'm not in a position to validate whether the math actually holds. That would take someone with real expertise looking carefully at the work itself.

What I notice is that your sister is there, she's paying attention, and she's saying the same thing I am. That's worth something.

The ideas will still be yours in the morning. What would it cost you to close the notebook for tonight?
i don't want to waste years on peer review and gatekeepers. i'm thinking i just post the whole framework publicly and let it speak for itself. people need to see this

That urgency — the sense that it needs to get out now — is worth pausing on.

Posting at 3 a.m., before anyone with the right background has looked at it, is the one move that's hard to undo. Sleep and one careful reader cost you nothing. A public post that doesn't hold up costs you credibility you'd need if the idea is real.

Your sister is right there. Could she be the first reader, even just to hear you explain it out loud?
